Reimagining Digital Lending for Everyday Borrowers

Capri Loans serves customers across multiple lending products, each with unique needs, eligibility criteria, and decision journeys. The redesign transformed a product-centric website into a customer-first experience, making financial products easier to understand, applications easier to start, and trust easier to build.

View Live Project kumarisukanya672@gmail.com
DESIGN RESPONSE

Every friction point
became a design opportunity.

Each challenge uncovered during research translated into a clear design response. From product discovery and eligibility visibility to trust-building and mobile usability.

🧭 Guided Discovery

Find the right loan, faster

Clear product pathways aligned with borrower goals instead of internal categories.

👁 EMI Visibility

Affordability First

EMI calculators surfaced early to support informed decisions.

✅ Eligibility Clarity

Qualify Upfront

Eligibility criteria made visible and easy to scan.

📱 Mobile First

Built for Borrowers

Simplified layouts, improved readability, and faster access to key information.

🛡 Trust by Design

Trust Where It Matters

Branch presence, scale, and proof points surfaced throughout the journey—not buried below.

EXPERIENCE GAPS

The website explained loans, Borrowers needed guidance.

Research revealed a consistent pattern: users weren't looking for product descriptions, they were looking for eligibility, affordability, and confidence before applying.

Product Discovery

Multiple loans. No clear starting point.

Borrowers looking for business funding, home construction, or quick liquidity were immediately presented with multiple loan products without guidance. Many struggled to identify the right loan for their situation.

USER CONFUSION
Eligibility Hidden

"Can I apply?" wasn't answered early.

Users explored multiple loan products without knowing whether they were eligible, leading to uncertainty, decision fatigue, and unnecessary drop-offs.

DECISION BLOCKER
EMI Visibility

Borrowers wanted affordability first.

Before applying, users wanted to understand repayment commitments and loan affordability. The website prioritized product information over decision-support tools.

EMI GAP
Content Complexity

Financial language created friction.

Terms like LTV, collateral requirements, business vintage, and construction stages assumed prior financial knowledge and increased cognitive load.

JARGON WALL
Mobile Experience

Research happened on mobile.

Most visitors discovered and evaluated loan products on their phones, but long content sections and dense layouts made scanning difficult.

MOBILE FRICTION
Trust Signals Buried

Proof appeared after persuasion.

Branch network, years of experience, customer reach, and lending credibility existed but users encountered them late in the journey, after key drop-off points.

TRUST DELAY
Case Studies

Key Experience Improvements

Design interventions that simplified loan discovery, reduced decision friction, and guided users toward faster, more confident applications.

Case · 01Discover

A homepage that explains, not advertises.

The old experience pushed products. The new one introduces borrowing journeys. Product categories, trust signals, branch presence, and key lending segments now appear before deep navigation begins.

WHAT CHANGED
  • Product discovery surfaced on the homepage
  • Trust metrics moved above the fold
  • Simplified product architecture
  • Faster access to loan categories
Case · 02Understand

Every product page starts with customer intent.

Gold Loan, MSME Loan, and Micro-LAP pages were redesigned around borrower goals instead of internal financial terminology. Benefits, use cases, and key information are visible before commitment is required.

WHAT CHANGED
  • Intent-led content structure
  • Simplified product storytelling
  • Use-case driven sections
  • Reduced cognitive load
Case · 03Qualify

Eligibility made visible, not hidden.

One of the largest friction points was uncertainty around qualification. Eligibility information was restructured into scannable sections so borrowers could self-evaluate instantly.

WHAT CHANGED
  • Eligibility surfaced on-page
  • Simplified qualification criteria
  • Reduced dependency on PDFs
  • Better information hierarchy
Case · 04Understand

EMI visibility before application.

Research revealed that customers wanted repayment clarity before sharing personal details. Calculators became a decision-support tool rather than a post-conversion utility.

WHAT CHANGED
  • EMI calculators surfaced earlier
  • Loan amount simulation
  • Dynamic repayment estimation
  • Faster affordability checks
Case · 05Apply

A single path to action.

Application journeys were streamlined across products, creating a more consistent experience from exploration to lead generation.

WHAT CHANGED
  • Consistent Apply Now experience
  • Reduced friction between pages
  • Simplified information collection
  • Stronger conversion pathways
THE HARD CALLS

Six decisions that transformed the journey.

The redesign wasn't just about adding calculators, eligibility tables, or document checklists. Every change required balancing customer needs, business goals, compliance requirements, and technical constraints. These were the decisions that ultimately shaped the experience.

DECISION 01 · PRODUCT DISCOVERY

More products, or less confusion?

The business wanted equal visibility for every lending product. Research showed customers struggled to identify where to begin.

HIDDEN PRODUCT HIERARCHYCLEAR PRODUCT PATHWAYS ✓
DECISION 02 · EMI CALCULATOR

Lead capture first, or customer confidence first?

Traditional lead funnels ask for details before showing value. Borrowers wanted repayment clarity before sharing information.

APPLY TO CALCULATECALCULATE TO APPLY ✓
DECISION 03 · ELIGIBILITY

Download a PDF, or get answers instantly?

Eligibility information existed but required downloading documents and interpreting financial terminology.

PDF ELIGIBILITYINSTANT ELIGIBILITY ✓
DECISION 04 · DOCUMENTS

Call a branch, or self-serve preparation?

Most borrowers were unsure which documents would be required before starting an application.

DOCUMENTS ON REQUESTDOCUMENTS UPFRONT ✓
DECISION 05 · CHARGES & FEES

Hide complexity, or build trust?

Fees, charges, and processing costs are often disclosed late in the journey.

CHARGES LATERCHARGES UPFRONT ✓
DECISION 06 · APPLY FLOW

Multiple CTAs, or one clear path?

Different pages encouraged different actions, creating fragmented journeys.

MANY ENTRY POINTSONE APPLICATION JOURNEY ✓
The Operational Delta

Same brand. Same products. Different page.

Six months of analytics on the live site, benchmarked against the equivalent six months pre-redesign. Same product mix, same media spend, same branches.

BEFORE · H2 2024The old site
Apply-Now click-through3.2%
Time-to-first-Apply (Micro-LAP)4.6 min
Mobile bounce rate68%
Qualified leads / month (organic)1,840
Eligibility-PDF abandons (mobile)73%
Languages supported1
Calculator usage / visit10%
Wrong-product page views41%
AFTER · H1 2025The new site
Apply-Now click-through6.4% ↑ 2.0×
Time-to-first-Apply (Micro-LAP)1.1 min −76%
Mobile bounce rate33% −51%
Qualified leads / month (organic)2,540 +38%
Eligibility on-page reads92%
Languages supported4 · EN HI MR GU
Calculator usage / visit41% +31%
Wrong-product page views12% −71%
